hero.question: Trump announces end of military operations against Iran by April 21st?
hero.prob_take: At 12%, the market is pricing this as a fantasy scenario, implying Trump would need to reverse months of escalating rhetoric and military positioning in less than 5 months, which is extremely hard to price above a rounding error. The recent -11.5 point slide suggests the crowd is still calibrating after Trump's latest Iran statements, but watch whether any actual de-escalation moves (diplomatic overtures, troop withdrawals) emerge before April, because right now you're paying for a black swan.

daily_take.headline: Iran Deal Odds Collapse 11 Points, Smart Money Smells Escalation
daily_take.deck: Trump-Iran military operations odds tanked from 23% to 12% in 24 hours on $405K volume. The sharp read: markets are pricing in zero chance of a ceasefire announcement by April 21st. At 12%, you're basically betting on a miracle. The real play lives next door where ceasefire extension odds dropped 8.5pts to 43%, signaling traders think things get worse before they get better. Short the hope, long the chaos.

sidebar.headline: Ceasefire extension odds fell harder than the base case, suggesting markets doubt any deal holds past April.
sidebar.label: Ceasefire Extension: 43.0%

sidebar.headline: Rangers-Panthers bid jumped 6pts while oil and Iran headlines flooded the tape, classic risk-on noise.
sidebar.label: Rangers vs. Panthers: 52.5%

sidebar.headline: Hormuz traffic normalization at 13.5% is the real tell. Oil markets are pricing zero de-escalation this quarter.
sidebar.label: Hormuz Traffic Normal: 13.5%
